title: Gender Equalities: What Lies Ahead
abstract: It is hard to over-estimate the importance of the social sciences in informing us of deepening inequalities at work and how they are likely to play out as the economic crisis generated by the Covid-19 pandemic unfolds. Understanding the long-term impact of the pandemic requires us to think across the intersection of work and society. This is challenging work as part of taking an intersectional position means accepting that transmission thrives on inequality where occupation and occupational risk are often mapped onto class, race, ethnicity, sex and geography (Middleton et al., 2020). [...]
authors_text: Cotton, Elizabeth; Beauregard, T Alexandra; Keles, Janroj Yilmaz
